summary: 6W schema model (WHAT/WHERE/WHEN/WHO/HOW/WHY) for all memory types. Unifies recall, simplifies graph ingestion. Exploration, not locked.

content.decision: Every memory should answer six questions at the top level: WHAT, WHERE, WHEN, WHO, HOW, WHY. Type-specific content stays in the content block. This unifies recall across types and makes graph ingestion trivial.
content.context: Current schema has type-specific content shapes with no unified core. Recall logic needs type-specific handling. Session-logs carry context that memories lack. Logs are richer than memories for reconstructing what happened — the 6W model fixes this by moving context into memories.
content.why: Uniform query surface across all memory types. Recall becomes type-independent. Graph mapping is direct: each W maps to either a node property or an edge type. Session-logs simplify to pure volumetrics. Benefits Core immediately, graph-readiness is a bonus.
alternatives_considered: Keep type-specific schemas, add unified fields incrementally — rejected: half-measure, doesn't solve the recall uniformity problem
Merge memory + session-log into one record — rejected: conflates metrics with knowledge, better to enrich memories and simplify logs separately
impact: Schema change benefits Core independently of graph DB timeline
Session-logs shrink to volumetrics (duration, counts), narrative moves to memories
Dead fields in session-log (profile, pipeline_tier) removed
Next step: validate 6W mapping against 5-10 real memories from current KB
